paragraphs.body: My name is Mira Solenne and I’m a student-athlete at the University of Pittsburgh. I recently read and enjoyed, “"Are You Allowed to Criticize Simone Biles?",” which focused on the important idea that, you will don’t need to criticise people that work really hard, but in the same time to give opinions about what they shood do with their lifes.

I noticed how effectively you used excellent examples of what she did in her career, but at the same time especially how you did presented! The idea with the tree really got my eye and inspired me. The idea of the essay when it comes to informations, that you started in an easy way and after you came with more and more deep explications. The way that you finish it, in the same way you started completes the circle on this subject.

annotations.text: noticed how effectively you used excellent examples of what she did in her career, but at the same time especially how you did presented! The idea with the tree really got my eye and inspired me. The idea of the essay when it comes to informations, that you started in an easy way and after you came with more and more deep explications. The way that you finish it, in the same way you started completes the circle on this subject.
annotations.comment: Nice work discussing the author's work without making it sound false or forced.

paragraphs.body: As a student-athlete I understand hundred presents what are you talking about. The hate that people give to athletes it’s insane! I remember, before moving to America some of my coaches warned me about how much people give their opinion when it comes to sports. This pressure of being perfect I feel it every day. Even I want or not. Sometimes I feel that motivates me to prove the people around me the opposite of what they think about me.

annotations.text: remember, before moving to America some of my coaches warned me about how much people give their opinion when it comes to sports. This pressure of being perfect I feel it every day.
annotations.comment: This is a really interesting point, and seems worthy of further explanation--could you be more specific about one of the lessons a coach or other respected person taught you about the experience of being an athlete in the public eye?

paragraphs.body: But yes, a lot of time I feel judged, or I think that I’m not good enough. That at every mistake that I will do someone won't believe in me. I wonder, why did you want to express your opinion about this subject with a vulgar language? Did you think like this will be more powerful or make people madder then they are already? And at the same time, why this way of writing? Did you was thinking that will get people attention easier, based on their curiosity?
Thank you for sharing an essay that made me think about an issue I hadn’t considered before. I will be more aware of how athletes will behave in this situation and try to be a better human!!

annotations.comment: You're doing a great job, of communicating effectively with an author. It can be very difficult to blend academic and personal communication in one document, but you are doing it very smoothly and sincerely. I truly would love to see you develop your own experiences/observations more, so the author will get an even stronger sense of who you are and what their writing means to you.
